how_to_use_body: Swipe through each section's cards to study vocabulary with Quran & Sunnah references. Then switch to Quiz to test yourself.
rule_taa_marbuta_body: Words ending in ـة are usually feminine. Example: رُكْبَةٌ (knee) → feminine. This covers ~90% of nouns.
rule_paired_body: Body parts that come in pairs (eyes, ears, hands, legs) are usually feminine — even without ـة. Examples: عَيْنٌ, أُذُنٌ, يَدٌ, رِجْلٌ.
rule_exceptions_masc_body:
Four body parts in this lesson come in pairs yet are masculine. You must memorise them:

• حَاجِبٌ (ḥājibun) — Eyebrow
• كَتِفٌ (katifun) — Shoulder
• ذِرَاعٌ (dhirāʿun) — Arm / Forearm
• فَخِذٌ (fakhidhun) — Thigh
rule_exceptions_fem_body:
Seven body parts in this lesson are feminine despite NOT ending in ـة. Most follow the paired-parts pattern, but you should know them explicitly:

• عَيْنٌ (ʿaynun) — Eye
• أُذُنٌ (udhunun) — Ear
• يَدٌ (yadun) — Hand
• رِجْلٌ (rijlun) — Leg
• سَاقٌ (sāqun) — Shin
• قَدَمٌ (qadamun) — Foot
• أَسْنَانٌ (asnānun) — Teeth
rule_demonstrative_body: Arabic has two words for 'this' based on gender. هٰذَا (hādhā) for masculine: هٰذَا قَلْبٌ. هٰذِهِ (hādhihi) for feminine: هٰذِهِ يَدٌ. This is called المُطَابَقَة (agreement).
rq1_q: Words ending in ـة (taa marbuta) are usually…
rq2_q: Body parts that come in pairs are most likely…
rq3_q: Which is feminine WITHOUT taa marbuta?
rq4_q: The Arabic term for gender agreement is…
rq5_q: هٰذَا is used with which noun type?
rq6_q: Which is masculine despite coming in pairs?
